summary: Tested grndwork landing page end-to-end. Backend: 8/8 pytest tests pass against the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL covering GET /api/, POST /api/waitlist (happy path with persistence verified via count delta), 422 validation for invalid email, missing fields and graduation_year out of range (2023, 2036, 1999), and GET /api/waitlist/count. Frontend: landing page loads, all nav testids present and clickable (nav-problem, nav-features, nav-how-it-works, nav-vision), hero CTAs (hero-cta-primary, hero-cta-secondary) and header-join-btn render, all 4 feature cards render, waitlist form validation shows correct messages on empty submit and on invalid email, and the happy-path submission produces the success card (data-testid=waitlist-success) plus the toast 'You’re on the list. We’ll reach out soon.' (verified visually in screenshot). Data persistence to Supabase Postgres confirmed by waitlist count incrementing after POST.

minor.issue: logger is referenced inside join_waitlist exception handler but logger is defined AFTER app routes/include_router (line 136). If a DB exception fires during import-time route registration order, logger would be unresolved at module load is fine, but on actual exception the global is resolved at call-time so it works. Still, recommend moving logging.basicConfig + logger = ... to the top of the file for clarity.

issues: React console warning: 'Select is changing from uncontrolled to controlled'. Initialize the react-hook-form defaultValues for graduation_year and career_interest (e.g., '' empty string) so the shadcn Select stays controlled from first render.

action_items: (Optional polish) Initialize defaultValues for grad-year and career-interest selects in Waitlist.jsx to suppress the React 'controlled/uncontrolled' warning.
(Optional polish) Move logging.basicConfig and logger definition above route handlers in /app/backend/server.py (cosmetic; functionally fine because logger is resolved at call-time).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py join_waitlist swallows the original exception and returns generic 500 – acceptable, but logger is declared after the handlers; works at runtime but order-of-definition is confusing and lint-flag-prone.

database.py: ASYNC_DATABASE_URL = DATABASE_URL.replace(...) will crash at import time if DATABASE_URL is missing (None.replace). Consider failing fast with a clear message via os.environ['DATABASE_URL']. Currently the server starts successfully so DATABASE_URL is set, but a missing env will surface as AttributeError instead of KeyError.

Hero.jsx splits the headline across two <span> nodes. This is fine visually but breaks strict Playwright text=matchers – add a single accessible heading testid (e.g., data-testid='hero-headline') for easier QA.
No DB connection cleanup on FastAPI shutdown for the SQLAlchemy engine – only Mongo client is closed. Add engine.dispose() in the shutdown handler.
seed_data_creation: Each pytest run inserts one TEST_-prefixed waitlist row (test_waitlist_post_valid_persists). No cleanup endpoint exists; rows accumulate in Supabase but are clearly tagged with TEST_ name and example.com email.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend public URL: https://future-launch-4.preview.emergentagent.com. All /api/waitlist endpoints functional and persisting to Supabase Postgres. Frontend waitlist form fully working including validation, select dropdowns, success card and toast. Pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_waitlist.py is reusable for regression. Only minor cosmetic items remain (uncontrolled/controlled Select warning, logger ordering).
